description: Per-claim faithfulness evaluation: all factual claims in the answer were checked against the cited source documents. Score = supported_claims / total_claims.

scores.notes: Muskogee RPO and mailing address (P.O. Box 8888, 74402-8888) both confirmed in article 554400000073486 Figure 1.02.
$45,000 threshold for four-signature review on a single school payment confirmed in Figure 1.06 of article 554400000073486.
50% of national average BAH and effective date October 1, 2011 both confirmed in article 554400000078677.
Rate of pursuit 9/12 = 0.75 confirmed; rounds to 80% BAH confirmed in MHA table in article 554400000078677.
730-909 days = 80% confirmed in benefit level chart; 70/80 rule noted with training inclusion confirmed in article 554400000073736.
40% benefit level eliminated August 1, 2020 and became 50% confirmed in article 554400000073736.
No delimiting date for veterans discharged on or after January 1, 2013; PL 115-48; effective August 18, 2017 — all confirmed in article 554400000073736.

Title 32 AGR qualifying effective October 1, 2011; Section 502(f) activations for national emergencies qualifying; retroactive payment for enrollments on or after August 1, 2009 — all confirmed in article 554400000073736.

Bartending courses precluded under all circumstances (Section 7.01a) confirmed in article 554400000075993; claim that this applies 'under all VA education benefit chapters' confirmed by section heading '7.01 Courses Precluded Under All Circumstances'.
Private pilot's certificate training prohibited as vocational flight (7.01d); exception for IHL credit toward college degree confirmed by reference to 38 USC 3680A(b) in article 554400000075993.

Second-class medical certificate required for non-ATP flight confirmed; must hold on first day of training confirmed; expires at end of 12th month (valid 12 months) confirmed — all in article 554400000073476.
Chapter 35 and Section 901 NOT eligible for vocational flight training confirmed in article 554400000073476 (sections 210b and 208d).
9-11 credit hours = 3/4 time when 12 is full time confirmed in article 554400000073534 table 6.04.
10-12 credits = 3/4 time when 13+ is full time confirmed in article 554400000073534 table 6.05.
BDN training time code 4 for full-time enrollment under Chapters 30, 32, 35, 1606 confirmed in article 554400000073534 Section 6.03 table.
Intervals not payable effective August 1, 2011 and citation to Section 206, PL 111-377 both confirmed in article 554400000073579.
Summer-term interval payable even if >30 days when summer term is at least 8 weeks long confirmed in article 554400000073579 Section 8.02c.
DEA (Dependents' Educational Assistance) folder described as 'two-part blue paper folder' confirmed in article 554400000073486.
EPC 270 suspense date = 60 days; other EPCs = 30 days confirmed in article 554400000073486.
4-year ROTC obligated period excluded from qualifying service confirmed in article 554400000078114.
5-year exclusion from date of commissioning for Service Academy graduates confirmed in article 554400000078114.
Maximum 60% of established tuition and fees for one term confirmed in article 554400000076342 Section 11.02.
OJT rates: First 6 months=100%, Second 6 months=80%, Third=60%, Fourth=40%, >24 months=20% confirmed in article 554400000078677.
EPC 240 for original Chapter 35 claim confirmed in article 554400000061193.
EPC 280 for Chapter 1606 supplemental confirmed in article 554400000061193.
QA scorecard Questions 8-10 cover payment confirmed in article 554400000059332.

unsupported_claims: October 1, 2023 as transition date from SAO to SOAR
VA Education shifted from SAO to SOAR for internal control assessments
scores.notes: Neither 'SOAR' nor 'SAO' nor this transition date appears anywhere in article 554400000059332 (M22-3 Chapter 2). The acronyms and date are completely absent from the source document.
All five operations (getPerson, getContactInfo, findPeopleByCriteria, getServicePeriods, getKickerInfo) confirmed in VADIR ICD Section 2.2 and Table 3.
'Payload shall be returned in no more than 2 seconds' confirmed in VADIR ICD Section 2.8.
Confidentiality=High, Integrity=High, Availability=Moderate, FIPS 199, and NIST SP 800-60 all confirmed in VADIR ICD Section 2.9.
SSN required, 9-digit string, may have leading zeros — all confirmed in VADIR ICD Table 7.
Default maxResults = 50, hard cutoff = 200 confirmed in VADIR ICD Table 7.
Code 'J' = 'Honorable for VA Purposes (Administrative use by VA only)' confirmed in VADIR ICD Table 42.
Project code 9GY = Hurricane Katrina (Aug 31, 2005), Training Indicator = N confirmed in VADIR ICD Table 43.
Code 'S' = Space Force confirmed in VADIR ICD Table 45.

unsupported_claims: Purple Heart information returned by getServicePeriods (this is in the getServicePeriods response but as a distinct element — the source does confirm purpleHeart is part of the getServicePeriods response structure)
Military Academy periods explicitly named as returned data
scores.notes: Section 2.2 confirms most data elements. Service periods, exclusion/training/active periods, Ch30 eligibility, Ch1606 eligibility, CIP, duty status are all confirmed. Military Academy periods and Purple Heart are present in the response tables but Section 2.2 narrative description is not fully explicit — partial support.
Production URL https://vavdrappprd.va.gov/vdrCH33/v2/VSCH33Service?WSDL confirmed in VADIR ICD Section 5.1.
MGSR status 'CA' = Suspended; description 'Completed requirements of baccalaureate or equivalent degree' confirmed in VADIR ICD Table 51.
exclusionPerdTyp values 'ROTC' or 'APPELLATE' confirmed in VADIR ICD Table 24.
Error 16820001 and callStatusNote text 'Invalid Search Criteria. Verify input value for SSN/EDIPI' confirmed in VADIR ICD Table 41.

unsupported_claims: 50%-100% benefit level range for 'days of service' — source shows 50% starts at 90 days not as the lower bound of a continuous scale described this way
scores.notes: 4-year ROTC exclusion confirmed; exclusionPerdTyp='ROTC' confirmed; reviewing getServicePeriods response confirmed; benefit level chart confirmed. The 50-100% range is generally accurate but the exact framing of the tiers may not perfectly match the source's presentation.
10 years from date of discharge for Chapter 32 confirmed in article 554400000073604.

Ozer v. Principi — surviving spouses have no delimiting date; regular Chapter 35 spouses do have delimiting date; restored by PL 107-103 effective December 28, 2001 — all confirmed in article 554400000073604.
$1,200 MGIB refund (96R) payable under Chapter 33 confirmed in article 554400000073421 (reference to Part 4, 13.23).
RPO having jurisdiction over the primary (degree-conferring) school retains jurisdiction confirmed in article 554400000073421.
Transaction code 06L = Work study allowance confirmed in article 554400000289086.
July 8, 2024 transition date from BDN to eMPWR for Chapter 33 confirmed in article 554400000291771.
DGI record lock releases after 15 minutes of inactivity confirmed in article 554400000303799.
PL 116-315 Section 1010 confirmed; NCD Phase 1 August 1, 2021 confirmed; IHL Phase 2 December 17, 2021 confirmed — all in article 554400000191366.

unsupported_claims: The precise ordering of all five operations (findPeopleByCriteria first, then getPerson, getContactInfo, getServicePeriods, getKickerInfo) as a defined sequential flow is not explicitly specified in Section 2.2 or 2.5 of the VADIR ICD
scores.notes: The five operations and their data contents are confirmed. The eligibility calculation steps (qualifying days, exclusions, benefit level, delimiting date) are confirmed in M22-4. The specific call order is implied by logic but not explicitly documented as a sequence in the cited source.

Code H = 'Under honorable conditions (absence of a negative report)' confirmed in Table 42. Codes A, B, H, J as qualifying confirmed in Table 42 and Section 6.1.2. Answer groups 'B' (Under honorable conditions, general) with H — source confirms both qualify. Minor: source lists more codes (A, B, H, J all qualify); answer accurately captures these.
9GF = OCO confirmed in Table 43; Training Indicator = N confirmed; active duty in support of contingency operation after September 10, 2001 as qualifying confirmed in M22-4 Sub-Chapter 2.

trainingPeriod with beginDate, endDate, projectCode confirmed in Table 25. Entry-level training deduction from Sub-Chapter 5 confirmed. 730-day threshold for whether training is deducted confirmed. Cross-reference procedure is implied by combining sources — well-supported synthesis.

unsupported_claims: mgibSeparationCode as a field name — the VADIR ICD Table 23 shows mgibCd and mgibPgmSepCd (not 'mgibSeparationCode')

When a veteran's Chapter 33 entitlement exhausts, VCE must check eligibility under other benefits including Chapter 30 — this specific procedure is in M22-4 Part 4 Chapter 1, which is correctly cited, but the field name is wrong
scores.notes: mgibCd confirmed in Table 23. The $1,200 MGIB refund (96R) connection to Chapter 30 eligibility confirmed. However, the field name 'mgibSeparationCode' does not match the ICD; the ICD uses 'mgibPgmSepCd' (MGIB Program Separation Code).
100% benefit level for Purple Heart recipients confirmed in M22-4 Sub-Chapter 2. purpleHeartIndCd = 'Y' confirmed in Table 28. Length-of-service override confirmed.

eduPgmEntlDelimitDt in bdnEnt (Table 30) confirmed. 10-year Ch30/32, 15-year Ch33 pre-2013, no delimiting date Ch33 post-Jan 1 2013 — all confirmed across source documents. PL 115-48 reference confirmed.

ch1606Ent fields (mgsrStatCd, mgsrStatCdDsc, mgsrStatCdRsn, mgsrStatEffDt, pnlCatCd) confirmed in Table 32. MGSR codes BA=Eligible/initial period, CA=Suspended/completed degree, DA=Terminated/failed to reaffiliate confirmed in Table 51.

unsupported_claims: If the veteran is using Chapter 33, the kicker is paid as a monthly supplement — the VADIR ICD does not describe how kicker payments are applied under Chapter 33; this claim goes beyond what the cited source states
scores.notes: kickerRateCode B1 description and OBSOLETE status confirmed in Table 46. beginDate/endDate and eligibilityStatus fields in KickerInfo confirmed in Table 40. The application as monthly supplement under Chapter 33 is not in the cited VADIR ICD source.

militaryStatus as two-digit code confirmed in Table 22. activeServiceIndicator 'N' = not in active Federal service confirmed. MHA eligibility for non-active-duty veterans enrolled above half-time confirmed in M22-4 Sub-Chapter 4.3. Active duty members not eligible for MHA confirmed.
svcCd 'F' = Air Force confirmed in Table 45. Statute code 'A' = Section 688 of 10 U.S.C. confirmed in Table 44.

unsupported_claims: QA Question 6 explicitly covers 'separation reason' as an eligibility review dimension — the source lists 'character of discharge' and 'delimiting date' and 'alternative elections' but 'separation reason' as a named field is not explicitly stated in the article text reviewed
scores.notes: Question 6 covers Eligibility in M22-3 QA scorecard confirmed. Character of discharge and delimiting date review confirmed. VADIR data elements (service periods, character of service, exclusion periods, Purple Heart) confirmed as relevant inputs to Question 6 evaluation.

EPCs 200/220/240/260 cover Chapters 30/32/35/1606 respectively confirmed in M22-3 Appendix A. No Chapter 33 EPC in the table confirmed. LTS calling getServicePeriods for service data confirmed in VADIR ICD Section 2.2. The claim that LTS 'automates what the EPC-based workflow handles manually' is a reasonable synthesis.

unsupported_claims: eduPgmTypCd '2' maps to a program type — the VADIR ICD Table 30 shows eduPgmTypCd as a field but the value '2' and its meaning are not explicitly defined in the tables reviewed
scores.notes: eduPgmEntlMtsQy = 24 meaning 24 months remaining confirmed; max 36 months confirmed in M22-4. Verification against TIMS/LTS confirmed as procedure. The eduPgmTypCd '2' meaning is not explicitly confirmed in source.

militaryAcademyPeriod fields (beginDate, endDate, branchOfService, commissionBranchOfService, svcCd) confirmed in Table 34. 5-year exclusion from commissioning date confirmed in M22-4 Sub-Chapter 5. West Point example (May 27, 2003 → May 26, 2008) confirmed in article 554400000078114.

unsupported_claims: VADIR Tables 52 (PGIB codes) referenced — Table 52 was not fully confirmed from sources reviewed; partial support only
scores.notes: pgibCd and pgibSeparationCode in Table 23 confirmed. mgad_loss_cat_codes code '01' = service connected disability confirmed in Table 48. 30+ days of active duty after September 10, 2001 for SC disability discharge qualifying at 100% confirmed in M22-4 Sub-Chapter 2.

cip fields (cipInd Y/N/X, cipBgnDt, cipEndDt) confirmed in Table 36. CIP periods as non-active-duty supported by M22-4 eligibility rules. The specific statement that 'CIP periods should not count toward qualifying service' is implied by M22-4 Sub-Chapter 2 (which defines qualifying service as active duty) but not explicitly stated about CIP.

unsupported_claims: VCE must issue a Chapter 33 Election Letter Template — the specific letter template name is not explicitly confirmed in the cited sources
scores.notes: ch30EnrlStatCd '03' = Participant confirmed in Table 50. mgadBprAm as Basic Pay Reduction Amount confirmed. Election as irrevocable confirmed in M22-4. $1,200 contribution refundable as 96R confirmed.

DGI search workflow (Ch33 database → VADIR findPeopleByCriteria → Create New or New Search) confirmed in article 554400000303799. findPeopleByCriteria requires SSN, returns up to 50/200 results confirmed in Table 7. callStatus 'Success' with 'Person Not Found' meaning — the specific note text was not explicitly confirmed but the concept is supported by Table 41.

unsupported_claims: Minimum 90 days active duty for eligibility — the answer states '90 days active duty after September 10, 2001' but M22-4 Sub-Chapter 2 shows the minimum is actually 90 days (for 50% level) but the answer conflates eligibility minimum with benefit level minimum threshold
scores.notes: Buffalo RPO handles foreign schools confirmed in article 554400000073486. Buffalo P.O. Box 4616, 14240-4616 confirmed. VADIR getServicePeriods for eligibility verification confirmed. MHA for foreign school = national average BAH confirmed in M22-4 Sub-Chapter 4.3. charSvcCd codes A, B, H for honorable confirmed in Table 42.

resultsHash as SHA-256 fingerprint of legacy and new values confirmed in Table 22. Change meaning DoD/DMDC updated the record confirmed. Re-evaluation of eligibility using updated data confirmed by M22-4 procedures.

unsupported_claims: Nonstandard term formula 'enrolled hours × 18 ÷ weeks in term' — the formula in article 554400000073534 Section 6.06 uses 'credit hours × 18 ÷ weeks' specifically for semester-hour schools; the '18' factor is specific to semester-hour basis, not universal
scores.notes: QA Questions 8-10 cover payment confirmed. Nonstandard term conversion formula confirmed (with minor qualification). Equivalency tables in Chapter 14 referenced. BDN training time code use confirmed. QA reviewer verifying correct table applied is a reasonable description.

eduLvlCd '51' = Baccalaureate confirmed in Table 49. MGSR code 'CA' = 'Suspended — Completed requirements of baccalaureate or equivalent degree' confirmed in Table 51. Ch 1606 with suspended status paused (not terminated) confirmed.

Chapter 1606 three signatures $8,000-$11,999.99 confirmed in Figure 1.06. Four signatures for >= $12,000 confirmed. ch1606Ent with mgsrStatCd B-prefix (BA/BB etc. = Eligible) confirmed in Tables 32 and 51. pnlCatCd for military component confirmed.

pgibCd and pgibSeparationCode in Table 23 confirmed. 30+ days qualifying for 100% if discharged for SC disability or sole survivorship confirmed in M22-4 Sub-Chapter 2. PGIB separation code informing whether 30-day exception applies confirmed as reasonable synthesis.

Title 32 AGR qualifying effective Oct 1, 2011 confirmed. Section 502(f) activations qualifying confirmed. Statute codes N, P, Q for Title 32 confirmed in Table 44. Project code A26 = AD-ADOT-AGR confirmed in Table 43. eMPWR replaced BDN as of July 8, 2024 confirmed. The answer is well-supported synthesis across three sources.

unsupported_claims: The 70/80 rule as described (aggregate including training = 730-909 days AND aggregate excluding training = 545-729 days → 70%) — this specific dual-condition framing goes beyond what was explicitly confirmed in the source; the 70/80 rule is in the source but the exact condition logic was not fully verified in the read excerpts
scores.notes: VADIR providing raw active/training period dates but not making eligibility decisions confirmed. Character of service edge cases (code H, blank defaults) confirmed in VADIR ICD. Title 32 AGR qualification requiring VCE judgment confirmed by M22-4 language. PGIB separation code ambiguity confirmed.

unsupported_claims: No automated trigger in the current architecture: the change is only detected on the next getServicePeriods call — this is a reasonable inference but is not explicitly stated in the cited sources
scores.notes: resultsHash change detection confirmed. M22-4 Part 4 Ch 1 VCE review requirement when new service data surfaces confirmed. M22-3 QA monthly sampling confirmed. The absence of an automated re-evaluation trigger is an accurate inference from the architecture but not explicitly stated.

VADIR 2-second SLA confirmed. Availability=Moderate (not High) confirmed. Error codes 16820002-16820005 for VADIR-side failures confirmed. BDN-to-eMPWR July 8, 2024 boundary confirmed. DMDC as source of truth (VADIR as downstream copy) confirmed in VADIR ICD Section 2.1.

Blank/unknown character of service defaults to honorable confirmed in VADIR ICD Section 6.1.2. Training Indicator Y = training confirmed in Table 43 note. Statute code 'Z' = Unknown confirmed in Table 44. These as data quality risks is supported synthesis.

unsupported_claims: QA scorecard has 13 questions — the source (M22-3 Chapter 2 article 554400000059332) clearly shows 16 questions across 7 categories (Q1-3 Data Integrity, Q4-5 Development, Q6 Eligibility, Q7 Entitlement, Q8-10 Payment, Q11-13 Notification, Q14-16 Systems Compliance)
Questions 14-16 (Systems Compliance) are omitted from the answer's description
M22-3 Chapter 2 mentions the transition from SAO to SOAR (October 1, 2023) — 'SOAR' and 'SAO' do not appear in the source article
scores.notes: Questions 8-10 covering Payment confirmed. Question 6 covering Eligibility confirmed. Monthly sampling confirmed. The '13 questions' claim is factually wrong per source — source shows 16 questions. The SOAR/SAO reference is unsupported.

VADIR 2-second SLA confirmed. SOAP over HTTPS with mutual TLS confirmed. All five WSDL operations confirmed. DGI search VADIR when no Ch33 record confirmed. 15-minute record lock timeout confirmed. eMPWR dependency confirmed. Well-supported synthesis.

unsupported_claims: 48-month rule (38 U.S.C. § 3695) caps combined entitlement across programs — this claim about VADIR's bdnEnt having 'no cross-program balance' is an inference about system design, not stated in the VADIR ICD
scores.notes: ch1606Ent MGSR codes confirmed. bdnEnt tracking entitlement per program confirmed. ch1606 'BA' = Eligible initial period confirmed. Election irrevocable confirmed. Benefit level under Ch33 based on active-duty (not drilling) days confirmed in M22-4 Sub-Chapter 2.

'More codes can and will be added as the DoD and DMDC see fit' confirmed in VADIR ICD Section 6. New codes transfer but descriptions won't until lookup table updated confirmed in Section 2.3. Unrecognized project codes appearing as raw codes without descriptions confirmed. Policy/M22-4 lag as a risk is a valid inference.

resultsHash change on re-pull confirmed. No scheduled re-pull (only on new enrollment triggering LTS) is a reasonable inference from architecture. M22-4 eligibility review at exhaustion confirmed. M22-3 QA monthly sample confirmed. NOD = EPC 270 with 60-day suspense confirmed. Well-supported.

unsupported_claims: Specific claim that 'SOAP/WSDL interface' would be replaced by 'event-driven integration' — this is a recommendation, not a factual claim from the source; the source describes the current SOAP architecture
scores.notes: This is a consulting/synthesis query. Current SOAP over HTTPS architecture confirmed. All five operations confirmed. Code tables with unknown pathway risk confirmed from Section 6. M22-4 eligibility rules confirmed. M22-3 QA as automated validation framing is synthesis. The architecture recommendation is the answer's own analysis, appropriate for consulting_sme type.

Error codes 16820003/16820005 for timeout/general errors confirmed in Table 41. 2-second SLA confirmed. DGI phantom record locks from VADIR hanging confirmed (15-min timeout from article 554400000303799). All five WSDL operations confirmed. Suspending claims pending resolution is reasonable procedure.

resultsHash consistency check confirmed. 50% as minimum level with zero qualifying days is consistent with benefit level chart. purpleHeartIndCd stuck to 'Y' scenario supported by Table 28. eMPWR transaction history review confirmed. M22-3 QA Question 6 retroactive review confirmed.

unsupported_claims: findPeopleByCriteria searching 'VADIR's person data' vs getServicePeriods querying 'a different data set' — this distinction (identity vs service history being separate datasets) is implied by the two different operations but not explicitly stated as such in the ICD
scores.notes: DGI Create New Chapter 33 Record when VADIR has no data confirmed. callStatus 'Success' with 'No Data Found' confirmed. EDIPI mismatch as possible cause is reasonable. Multiple SSN/state system sync scenarios are plausible inferences.

Error 16820004 = transformation error confirmed in Table 41. 'Contact VADIR Services Support' as prescribed action confirmed. Distinction from 16820001 (invalid query) and 16820003 (database error) confirmed. Section 6 note about DMDC adding codes unilaterally confirmed.

BDN-to-eMPWR boundary July 8, 2024 confirmed. VCEs can no longer CEST/CADJ in BDN but can view historical records confirmed. No transactions after July 8 in BDN confirmed. Active receivables migrated to eMPWR confirmed — all in article 554400000291771.

unsupported_claims: ServicePeriod element allows 'zero to many' activePeriods (Table 23) — this specific cardinality statement ('zero to many') was not explicitly confirmed in the tables reviewed
scores.notes: Payload size growth indicating more service periods is a reasonable inference. VADIR 2-second SLA at risk from larger payloads is consistent with Section 2.8. DGI screens potentially slower confirmed. LTS eligibility iterating over active periods is a reasonable inference. Monitoring p95 and alerting VADIR Support are appropriate procedures.

unsupported_claims: OLAF screens E01/E04 showing approval status — OLAF screens referenced in M22-4 Part 4 Chapter 6 were not directly confirmed in sources reviewed; this is a cross-reference that was not verified
scores.notes: Authorization error steps (exit/retry, BGS/address errors, Senior/Lead, data remediation POC, suspend token) confirmed in article 554400000291771. School-side causes (facility code, address mismatch) are reasonable inferences. RPO Support Services Division confirmed. RPO jurisdiction for school determining which SSD handles it is confirmed.

Three environments (Dev/Test/PIT, PreProd, Production) with URLs confirmed in Section 5.1. Section 7.4 explicitly addressing DR URL change confirmed. All five WSDL operations confirmed. Mutual TLS with SHA2 encryption confirmed in Section 3.1.6. resultsHash check post-failover confirmed.

unsupported_claims: SOAR replacing SAO October 1, 2023 — as noted for Q27 and Q80, 'SOAR' and 'SAO' do not appear in M22-3 Chapter 2 source article
scores.notes: QA Question 6 covering eligibility confirmed. 70/80 rule and exclusion periods as training vs. policy errors confirmed. eMPWR July 8, 2024 and 40% elimination August 1, 2020 as policy change examples confirmed. VADIR callStatus and resultsHash for data issue triage confirmed. M22-3 Questions 1-3 (Data Integrity) confirmed.

eMPWR for all Ch33 BTs on/after July 8, 2024 confirmed. DGI/VADIR independence from eMPWR for eligibility confirmed. BT ordering (overpayments first, then payments) confirmed in article 554400000291771. 15-minute DGI record lock confirmed. Suspending TIMS tokens pre-maintenance confirmed.

unsupported_claims: 48-month rule does NOT apply to Chapter 1607 + National Call to Service combinations — this specific exception was not explicitly confirmed in sources reviewed
scores.notes: Ch33 max 36 months confirmed in M22-4 Part 12. 48-month combined entitlement rule (38 U.S.C. § 3695) confirmed. bdnEnt tracking entitlement under multiple programs confirmed. The VCE checking bdnEnt for other program entitlement used confirmed.

VCEs cannot CEST/CADJ Ch33 in BDN as of July 8, 2024 confirmed. VCEs CAN view historical BDN records from before conversion confirmed. No transactions after July 8 in BDN confirmed. Both BDN (historical) and eMPWR (post-July 8) being needed confirmed — all in article 554400000291771.

Entry-level training deducted only if veteran has fewer than 730 days total service confirmed in M22-4 Sub-Chapter 5. 30-day SC disability discharge qualifying at 100% regardless of training deductions confirmed. 730-day threshold as the resolution to the apparent contradiction confirmed.

40% eliminated August 1, 2020 → 50% confirmed. Monthly enrollment verification August 1, 2021 (NCD)/December 17, 2021 (IHL) confirmed. eMPWR replacing BDN July 8, 2024 confirmed. PL 115-48 delimiting date elimination for post-Jan 1, 2013 separations confirmed. Re-pulling service record data as recommended procedure is reasonable.

Pre-Jan 1, 2013 discharge = 15-year delimiting date confirmed. 2011 separation = ~2026 delimiting date analysis confirmed. Delimiting date extension types (later period of service, disability, other qualifying reasons per Figure 4.01) confirmed in article 554400000073604.

PL 116-315 Section 1010 confirmed. Phase 1 August 1, 2021 NCD confirmed. Phase 2 December 17, 2021 IHL confirmed. Initial + second certification requirement confirmed. Monthly verification by beneficiary on same phased timeline confirmed — all in article 554400000191366.

unsupported_claims: VCE uses the FOCAS system for correspondence training — FOCAS (Flight, On-the-Job Training, Correspondence, and Apprentice System) is referenced in the answer but not confirmed in sources reviewed for this article

Chapter 33 correspondence training reimbursed 'per the correspondence training rate rather than the standard tuition/fee structure' — specific Ch33 correspondence rate structure not confirmed in sources reviewed
scores.notes: Correspondence restrictions (50%+ students need 6+ months, approved programs, nonaccredited prohibited since Feb 2, 1995) confirmed in M22-4 Part 3 Ch 7. Monthly enrollment verification not applicable to correspondence confirmed in article 554400000191366. No MHA for correspondence-only is consistent with Sub-Chapter 4.3 (MHA requires physical attendance above half-time).

Benefit level as mathematical determination from qualifying days confirmed. NOD = EPC 270 with 60-day suspense confirmed. Re-pulling service records and recalculating as first path confirmed. VCE applying exclusion rules (ROTC, Service Academy, training deductions) as challengeable confirmed.

unsupported_claims: VET TEC provides 'Chapter 33-level MHA' but operates under different rules — VET TEC reference is mentioned in M22-4 Part 12 Chapter 11 but its MHA equivalence claim was not explicitly confirmed in sources reviewed
scores.notes: SAA approval required for standard Ch33 confirmed. Unapproved courses prohibited confirmed in M22-4 Part 3 Ch 7. VET TEC as separate high-tech program confirmed by reference in article 554400000191366. Checking SAA approval or VET TEC provider status as the correct procedure is well-supported.

unsupported_claims: LTS has a VA-ONCE inbox for unpayable enrollments that VCE must check when entitlement exhausts — this specific detail about an LTS VA-ONCE inbox was referenced but not directly confirmed in sources reviewed
scores.notes: LTS as the consuming system calling VADIR confirmed in VADIR ICD Sections 1-2. Five VADIR operations called by LTS confirmed. DGI workflow using LTS for underlying eligibility confirmed. LTS using VADIR data for eligibility/payment/disallowance confirmed.

BDN as master record pre-July 8, 2024 confirmed. CEST/CADJ commands confirmed. eMPWR as effective master record for Ch33 post-July 8, 2024 confirmed. BDN remaining master record for Chs 30, 32, 35, 1606, 901 confirmed. DMDC/VADIR as source of truth for military service history confirmed.

charSvcCd code values (A, B, D, F, H, J, Y, Z) confirmed in Table 42. Ch33 requiring honorable service confirmed in M22-4 Sub-Chapter 2. Blank/unknown defaults to honorable confirmed in Section 6.1.2. QA Question 6 checking character of discharge confirmed. Blank-defaults-to-honorable risk not explicitly flagged in QA confirmed.

unsupported_claims: Certificate of Eligibility (COE) issued before school enrollment step — the timing of COE issuance within the flow (step 3 before school enrollment) is not explicitly sequenced this way in a single source; it is a reasonable inference
scores.notes: DGI search workflow (SSN → findPeopleByCriteria → EDIPI → getPerson) confirmed. getServicePeriods for active/exclusion/training periods, Purple Heart, Ch30/1606 entitlement confirmed. M22-4 Sub-Chapter 2 eligibility rules (90 days, honorable, excluded service, benefit level) confirmed. Two-certification requirement confirmed. MHA calculation confirmed. eMPWR payment authorization confirmed. Big-pay thresholds ($25K/$45K) confirmed. Monthly verification confirmed.

unsupported_claims: M22-3 QA reviews verify correct use of all these systems through '13-question scorecard' — as established, the source shows 16 questions, not 13
scores.notes: DGI confirmed. eMPWR with inquiry/read-only default role confirmed. BDN for historical and non-Ch33 benefits confirmed. TIMS for folder management confirmed. VA-ONCE for enrollment certification confirmed. SHARE for identity management confirmed. eFolder for claims file confirmed. MPI Toolkit for data remediation confirmed — all in various M22-4 sources.

unsupported_claims: Annual BAH rate update effective August 1 based on preceding January 1 DoD BAH changes — the specific 'August 1' update date with 'preceding January 1' mechanism was not explicitly confirmed in sources reviewed for Sub-Chapter 4.3
scores.notes: MHA based on E-5 with dependents BAH rate changing annually confirmed. DMDC corrections/additions to service periods triggering benefit level change confirmed. School tuition changes via second certification confirmed. QA catching errors (Questions 6, 8-10) and requiring correction confirmed. Legislative changes confirmed (40% elimination). Big-pay review ($25K/$45K) confirmed.

key_issues: Q27 (score 0.0): 'SOAR' and 'SAO' and the October 1, 2023 transition date do not appear anywhere in the cited source (M22-3 Chapter 2, article 554400000059332).
Q80 (score 0.5): Answer claims QA scorecard has '13 questions' but source clearly shows 16 questions across 7 categories — the Systems Compliance category (Questions 14-16) is omitted.
Q54 (score 0.7): Field name 'mgibSeparationCode' does not match the VADIR ICD field name 'mgibPgmSepCd' in Table 23.
Q102 (score 0.7): FOCAS system claim and Ch33 correspondence rate structure not confirmed in cited sources.
Q94 (score 0.8): References SOAR/SAO transition (October 1, 2023) which is unsupported in the cited source, same issue as Q27 and Q80.
Q109 (score 0.8): Repeats the '13 questions' error from Q80.
